arts.ttl: How many games Marc Cucurella will miss for Chelsea after red card vs Brentford
arts.sum: Therefore, Enzo Maresca will be without the left-back for next Sunday's Premier League clash away at Everton. Marc Cucurella will miss Chelsea's clash against Everton next weekend after being sent off against Brentford on Sunday.

arts.ttl: Marc Cucurella breaks silence with 12-word message after Chelsea red card vs Brentford
arts.sum: Marc Cucurella has broken his silence after he was shown a red card after the full-time whistle following Chelsea's win over Brentford. The defender was shown a second yellow card after the Blues' 2-1 win over the Bees on Sunday.

arts.ttl: Chelsea the weekend's big winners as Europe's giants stumble
arts.sum: How else could Liverpool, Arsenal, Manchester City, Barcelona, Real Madrid, Juventus and Bayern Munich all dropping points be explained? Santa Claus delivered pretty much the perfect weekend: a 2-1 win for the Blues against Brentford at Stamford Bridge, while rivals Liverpool, Arsenal, Manchester City and Aston Villa all dropped points.

arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca reveals why he feels Chelsea can't compete for the title despite moving just two points behind Liverpool with win over Brentford
arts.sum: The Blues secured a 2-1 win at home to Brentford on Sunday to close the gap on league leaders Liverpool to just two points, although Arne Slot's side have a game in hand.

arts.ttl: 'We are not ready' - but are Chelsea genuine title contenders?
arts.sum: Enzo Maresca keeps talking them down, but is it time to seriously consider Chelsea as genuine title contenders?

arts.ttl: Revealed: Why Marc Cucurella was sent off AFTER the full-time whistle in Chelsea's win over Brentford
arts.sum: The heated scenes continued after the final whistle and referee Peter Bankes booked Brentford forward Kevin Shade and showed Cucurella a second yellow.

arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca continues to play down Chelsea’s title chances despite latest win
arts.sum: The Blues moved to within two points of Premier League leaders Liverpool with Sunday’s victory over Brentford

arts.ttl: Brentford very close to 'deserved' draw - Frank
arts.sum: Boss Thomas Frank praises Brentford's performance and says a draw with Chelsea would have been "well deserved" as they lose 2-1 at Stamford Bridge.

arts.ttl: Marseca explains why Chelsea are still ‘not ready’ and gives updates on Felix and Lavia injuries
arts.sum: Chelsea reaction. The post Marseca explains why Chelsea are still ‘not ready’ and gives updates on Felix and Lavia injuries appeared first on West London Sport.

arts.ttl: Marc Cucurella masters ‘three positions’ to inspire Chelsea’s title charge
arts.sum: Chelsea 2-1 Brentford: The Spaniard’s all-action display helped the Blues close the gap on Liverpool to just two points at the top of the Premier League, though manager Enzo Maresca maintains his side are not in the title race
